charged.short: Non-votable expenditure charged on the Consolidated Fund of India.
charged.long: Outflows that Parliament cannot vote on — they are 'charged' on the Consolidated Fund. Typical items: interest on debt, salaries of constitutional posts (President, Judges, CAG), and court decrees. They are debated but not put to vote.

voted.short: Expenditure approved by Parliament through Demands for Grants.
voted.long: Every voted item is presented as a Demand for Grants and put to vote in the Lok Sabha. This is where Parliament exercises its power of the purse — most ministry spending is voted.

revenue.short: Recurring spending that does not create assets — salaries, subsidies, grants, interest.
revenue.long: Revenue expenditure is consumed in the year — it doesn't add to the government's assets. Examples: PM-KISAN cash transfer, fertiliser subsidy, salaries, pensions.

capital.short: Spending that creates assets or reduces liabilities.
capital.long: Capital expenditure builds something durable — infrastructure, equipment, equity in PSUs — or repays debt. Higher capital share usually signals investment intent.

grandTotal.short: Revenue Section + Capital Section, before recoveries.
grandTotal.long: The gross outflow proposed for a Demand. It sums every Major Head under the Revenue and Capital sections.

recoveries.short: Receipts adjusted against expenditure (e.g. cost recovered from states/users).
recoveries.long: Money flowing back into a Demand — fees, cost recovered from states, repayments of loans. Shown as a deduction so the net 'Expenditure Provision' reflects true outflow.

expenditureProvision.short: Grand Total − Recoveries. The net amount actually budgeted to be spent.
expenditureProvision.long: This is the figure that hits the Consolidated Fund net of recoveries. When recoveries are large relative to gross, the provision can be much smaller than the headline Grand Total.

majorHead.short: 4-digit functional code grouping spending by purpose (e.g. 2401 Crop Husbandry).
majorHead.long: The top of the LSMMH-DDOH-OH classification. Major Head 2401 is Crop Husbandry, 2415 is Agricultural Research, 3601 is Grants-in-aid to States. They are the same across Centre and States.

ddg.short: The line-item-level booklet a ministry publishes alongside the Budget.
ddg.long: DDGs break each Demand down to Sub-Head and Object-Head — the lowest layer of the budget. This is where you see PM-KISAN transfers vs. office expenses vs. subsidies.
